preamble: Spirantization (lenition) of uvular stop [q] to uvular fricative [χ], common in several Arabic and Turkic dialects.

languageExamples.note: Lenition of uvular stop to fricative in specific phonological environments.
Similar spirantization patterns observed in some Central Asian languages.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
